_meta.description: SEO cluster definitions. Every cluster must be grounded in at least one denial code or regulation. Drives programmatic SEO and internal linking.

tier_model.description: Three-tier internal link architecture introduced in SEO Gap Analysis v1 (src_0011). Pillar pages sit at top. Category hub pages are mid-tier. Denial code pages are the base layer.

tiers.pillar: Top-level evergreen pages. Whole-topic coverage. Everything links up to these.
tiers.category_hub: Mid-tier pages grouping denials by type (Administrative, Medical Necessity, Coverage/Network). Route users to the correct tool and link down to code pages.
tiers.code_cluster: Base-layer denial code pages and supporting service-specific pages. Link up to the relevant category hub.

records.name: Medical Necessity Denial Appeals
records.target_problem: Patient's health insurance denied a service as not medically necessary and they need to appeal.
records.notes: Highest priority cluster. CO-50 is the most searched denial code. 40-60% appeal win rate (KFF/OIG data through 2023). Primary referral intake surface at Month 6. Pillar page + denial-specific support pages. Phase 2 service expansion: MRI, PT, medications, sleep studies, mental health, emergency, DME, labs.

records.name: Prior Authorization Denials and Overrides
records.target_problem: Patient's service was denied because prior authorization was not obtained, or was denied after requesting.
records.notes: High search volume. Emergency exception angle is the unique GEO-optimized answer block. CO-151 (Authorization Required) is in the 12-code priority set.

records.name: Mental Health Insurance Parity Disputes
records.target_problem: Patient's mental health or substance use treatment was denied or covered at worse rates than physical health services.
records.notes: MHPAEA is the anchor law. Growing search volume post-COVID. Comparative analysis angle is differentiating. Phase 2 service cluster (flagged as missing in Gap Analysis). Strong referral potential under federal parity law.

records.name: Medical Bill Dispute and Negotiation
records.target_problem: Patient received an unexpected or incorrect medical bill and needs to dispute or negotiate it.
records.notes: No Surprises Act and balance billing protections are key content angles. Medical Bill Dispute Tool is the CTA. Administrative errors are the largest single denial category (50-60% per KFF data through 2023). Phase 2: add Claim Correction as a distinct SEO entry point (Gap 6 from SEO Gap Analysis v1).

records.name: Patient Rights and HIPAA Records Access
records.target_problem: Patient needs to understand their rights to access medical records or challenge healthcare decisions.
records.notes: HIPAA Records Request tool is the CTA. Good entry-point cluster for patients just starting to advocate.

records.name: How to Appeal an Insurance Denial — Pillar
records.target_problem: Patient received an insurance denial and does not know how to appeal it or where to start.
records.brand_stat: Fewer than 1% of patients appeal insurance denials, even though 40-60% of those who do win (KFF analysis of Healthcare.gov data through 2023). This stat anchors the pillar page, homepage hero, and all denial code page intro blocks.
records.notes: Top of the three-tier architecture. All hub pages and code pages link up here. This is the single highest-priority content asset in the business. Publish first. Brand stat must appear in the first 100 words. Requires all 7 EEAT trust pages to be attorney-reviewed before publish (MA-EEAT-001 gate).

records.name: Administrative Denial Category Hub
records.target_problem: Patient's claim was denied for paperwork, billing, or documentation reasons — not because of a medical decision.
records.notes: Mid-tier hub. Covers ~50-60% of all denials (KFF/CMS data through 2023). Code pages within this hub: CO-16 (missing/invalid info), CO-97 (bundled service), CO-18 (duplicate claim), CO-22 (coordination of benefits), CO-29 (timely filing), CO-45 (charge exceeds fee schedule). Routes to Medical Bill Dispute Tool. Administrative errors typically resolve with resubmission — not strong referral candidates. Links up to How to Appeal pillar (page_0006) and links down to all admin code pages.

records.name: Medical Necessity Denial Category Hub
records.target_problem: Patient's insurance denied a service as not medically necessary, or a prior authorization was denied, and they need to formally appeal.
records.notes: Mid-tier hub. Highest attorney referral potential of all three hubs. 40-60% appeal win rate (KFF/OIG data through 2023). Code pages within this hub: CO-50 (not medically necessary), CO-197/CO-151 (authorization required), and supporting prior auth pages. Phase 2 service expansion pages sit below this hub: MRI, PT, medications, sleep, mental health, emergency, DME, labs. This page is also the primary referral intake surface when Month 6 activates. Build Month 6 referral wiring in the draft now even though it will not activate until then.

records.name: Coverage and Network Denial Category Hub
records.target_problem: Patient's claim was denied because the service or provider is not covered under their plan, or is out of network.
records.notes: Mid-tier hub. Smallest volume of the three categories (10-15% of denials). Code pages within this hub: CO-96 (non-covered service), PR-204 (not covered under plan), CO-109 (wrong payer), CO-B7 (provider not certified). No Surprises Act content belongs here for emergency out-of-network denials. This hub is Phase 1 in the queue but lower priority than Administrative and Medical Necessity hubs. Referral potential is variable and depends on state law.

records.name: Cornerstone Guide Library
records.target_problem: Patients navigating the full spectrum of insurance denials, billing disputes, and patient rights need comprehensive, authoritative reference guides.
records.publish_sequence: Phase 1 (1 guide) → Phase 4 Wave 1 (2) → Wave 2 (3) → Wave 3 (3) → Wave 4 (1)
records.cornerstone_sequencing_rule: No cluster pages under any cornerstone topic may publish before the parent cornerstone is live and indexed. Violating this rule fragments topical authority.

cornerstones.title: The Complete Guide to Insurance Claim Denials
cornerstones.cluster_anchors: Denial codes, prior auth, claim processing

cornerstones.title: The Complete Guide to Medical Billing Disputes
Patient Rights in Hospitals: The Complete Guide
cornerstones.cluster_anchors: Patient bill of rights, discharge rights, EMTALA

cornerstones.title: The Complete Guide to Medicaid Appeals
The Complete Guide to Medicare Appeals
cornerstones.cluster_anchors: Medicare denials, prior auth, coverage gaps

cornerstones.title: How Prior Authorization Works: A Patient's Guide
The No Surprises Act & Surprise Billing: Complete Guide
Nursing Home Rights & Abuse Reporting: Complete Guide
How to File Complaints Against Hospitals & Insurers
Hospital Financial Assistance & Charity Care Guide
cornerstones.cluster_anchors: Charity care, financial assistance, medical debt

records.notes: Cornerstone guides are the authority backbone of the SEO strategy. All cluster pages inherit their trust signal from the parent cornerstone. Tier 3 content — requires highest review level (Kate editorial board sign-off mandatory before publication). Annual review cycle minimum. Any linked statute or regulatory change triggers immediate re-review queue. See dec_0010 for cornerstone sequencing rule. See MA-SEO-SUP-001 §1 for full production standard.

records.name: Policy Interpretation Content Cluster
records.target_problem: Patients need plain-language explanations of healthcare policy changes (Medicaid, Medicare, ACA, No Surprises Act) that affect their coverage and appeal rights.
records.publish_sequence: Phase 4 Wave 2 — Medicaid sub-cluster first (10–15 pages), then Medicare, ACA, Insurance Regulation, Patient Rights Regulation

compliance_rules.allowed: Explaining policy changes, summarizing government guidance, linking to official sources, directing users to legal aid organizations
compliance_rules.not_allowed: Advising users whether to enroll or withdraw from any program, interpreting immigration consequences for specific individuals, predicting enforcement outcomes, providing immigration legal strategy
compliance_rules.required_disclaimer: This page provides general information only. MyAdvocate is not a legal or immigration service. For advice about your specific situation, please consult a licensed attorney.
compliance_rules.freshness_sensitivity: high — regulatory changes trigger immediate re-review

sales_funnel.tool_cta_mapping: denials → Insurance Denial Fighter; billing → Medical Bill Dispute Tool; complaint → complaint filing resource
sales_funnel.email_capture: Get updates when this regulation changes opt-in module
sales_funnel.resource_connector: route to legal aid for topics requiring attorney guidance

surge_response_protocol.trigger: CMO Agent flags policy topic based on Ahrefs keyword movement or news signal
surge_response_protocol.surge_tag: surge-timing tag applied at publish for performance tracking

metrics_targets.review_cadence: 6 months minimum; any government source change triggers re-review

records.notes: Policy cluster is top-of-funnel. All existing SEO architecture rules apply. Each policy page must pass YMYL compliance review before publish. Freshness-sensitive classification — registered in Content Refresh Agent as highest-priority re-review tier. CMO Agent Surge Detection module (Phase 4) monitors this cluster for keyword spikes tied to regulatory news events. See MA-SEO-SUP-001 §5 for full compliance rules.

records.name: Spanish-Language Content Cluster
records.target_problem: Spanish-speaking patients are systematically underserved by healthcare navigation resources, particularly for Medicaid, billing disputes, and patient rights.
records.phase_gate: Phase 4 pilot (policy cluster only) → Phase 5 scale (full library)
records.publish_sequence: Phase 4 Wave 2: 10–15 Medicaid policy pages translated → Phase 5: scale to 100+ pages

priority_pages: how to appeal a Medicaid denial
pilot_scope.translation_standard: AI draft + human fluency review required before publication
pilot_scope.kate_review: applies equally to Spanish content (clinical framing, not language fluency)
pilot_scope.attorney_review: same rule as English — required for immigration-adjacent content
pilot_scope.attribution_block: same editorial board attribution, translated to Spanish

phase_5_scale_priorities.denial_code_pages_top_50: high — denial codes appear on EOBs received by Spanish speakers
phase_5_scale_priorities.medical_billing_dispute_guides: high — financial impact content has broad demographic demand
phase_5_scale_priorities.patient_rights_state_specific: medium — high value for Spanish-speaking hospital patients
phase_5_scale_priorities.cornerstone_guides: medium — begin with Medicaid and Medical Billing cornerstones
phase_5_scale_priorities.nursing_home_rights: high — elder care content is highly searched in Spanish

compliance_rules.note: All rules that apply to English content apply equally to Spanish content. This is not a lower-compliance track.

metrics_targets.tracking: tracked as separate segment in Search Console and Ahrefs

gamification.spanish_library_100_pages: +800 XP | Achievement: La Voz del Paciente

records.notes: Spanish-language content is a first-mover play in an underserved vertical — not a translation project. Phase 4 pilot validates search demand and review workflow before Phase 5 scale. CMO Agent Spanish Track (activated Phase 4) monitors Spanish keyword performance to inform Phase 5 scale decision. This cluster supplements MA-YT-001 Spanish channel strategy and MA-IG-001 Spanish content track. See MA-SEO-SUP-001 §8 for full strategy.
